HPE RFP Demo

Live RFP opportunity

-Revenue Intelligence Platform

Rehearse your upcoming RFP demo with HPE's senior finance and sales operations leadership in a realistic AI simulation. Practice handling CFO-level TCO interrogation, finance-digital-transformation scrutiny, multi-currency complexity objections, and driving toward RFP shortlist commitment with a room where every attendee scores 8+ on DISC Dominance.

⌄
⌄
What You're Practicing
Practice the upcoming HPE RFP Demo with 6 senior leaders spanning Finance, Sales Operations, Sales Analytics, and Digital Transformation. The room splits into two power centers — a finance-strategy bloc (Dave Serr, Gustav van der Westhuizen, Bobby Jutley — all DC/DI types demanding TCO proof, SOX compliance evidence, and payback period math) and a sales operations bloc (Andrew Schultz, Anne Bolton, Ranjan Chakraborty — wanting rep experience, adoption curves, and analytics integration). This simulation helps you survive CFO Dave Serr's 3-year TCO interrogation, navigate Bobby Jutley's finance-governance deep-dive on audit trails and multi-currency handling, activate Ranjan Chakraborty as your analytics champion, and position Aviso as a unified revenue intelligence platform — not another dashboard — to earn a spot on HPE's RFP shortlist within a tight 90-minute window.
Key Objectives

  • CFO TCO defense — Dave Serr

    Dave Serr DI is CFO of HPE's Server Business with a CPA background and deep M&A experience — he's mentally calculating total cost of ownership from the moment you speak. Anchor on the $55K three-year TCO breakdown (Year 1: $25K including implementation, Years 2–3: $15K/year). Prepare to articulate payback period, implementation timeline, and revenue leakage detection ROI. He'll ask about Sarbanes-Oxley compliance and financial reporting controls. Speak in operational finance language, not product language. Have the cost-benefit analysis ready before he asks for it.

  • Finance governance deep-dive — Bobby Jutley

    Bobby Jutley DC leads Finance Digital Transformation and has a Compliance score of 8/10. He will stress-test every governance claim. Proactively address audit trail capabilities, regulatory reporting features, data-governance alignment with HPE's internal finance policies, and multi-currency/multi-entity handling across HPE's global operations. Bobby drove HPE's RPA and AI adoption at scale — frame Aviso as the next evolution of his transformation mandate, not a competing initiative. Bring benchmarks from comparable enterprise deployments. No hand-waving survives this attendee.

  • COO strategic alignment — Gustav van der Westhuizen

    Gustav DC is COO of Finance & Strategy with deep experience in finance transformation and circular economy initiatives at HPEFS. He evaluates platform investments through a strategic roadmap lens — how does Aviso align with HPE's broader digital transformation goals? He'll ask about scalability guarantees, data growth projections, strategic KPI reporting to senior finance leadership, and cross-functional governance models. Position Aviso as infrastructure for HPE's finance-strategy roadmap, not a point purchase. Lead with the governance model recommendation.

  • Sales pricing & margin story — Andrew Schultz

    Andrew Schultz DI is SVP of Global Sales Finance & Pricing — the most senior attendee by title. With a DI profile (Dominance 9, Influence 8), he's fast-paced, strategic, and will challenge you on how Aviso impacts pricing strategy and margin optimization for server sales. Demonstrate financial modeling capabilities, scenario-based revenue forecasts, and discounting rules/approval workflows. Don't pitch features — pitch the strategic pricing advantage. Show what his Monday morning executive briefing looks like with Aviso's forecasting versus without it.

  • Champion activation — Ranjan Chakraborty

    Ranjan Chakraborty DS is VP of Sales Analytics and Insights — your strongest internal champion candidate. His mandate maps directly to Aviso's pipeline segmentation, forecasting engine, and BI integration capabilities. He has a DS profile (Steadiness 9) meaning he values long-term strategic alignment and won't make impulsive decisions. Arm him with a compelling analytics narrative he can sell internally: pipeline granularity improvements, accuracy percentages from prior deployments, and dashboard integration specifics. Offer a proof-of-concept with anonymized HPE pipeline data. His buy-in carries the analytics credibility the room needs.

  • Sales & partner experience — Anne Bolton

    Anne Bolton DC is VP of Global Sales and Partner Experience managing large teams and transformation programs. She thinks like a sales ops leader, not a buyer — she's evaluating adoption risk, not features. Address the user adoption curve for sales reps and partner teams, training delivery model, co-selling workflow support, and how real-time insights surface within the rep's existing workflow. Show her the "before and after" of a rep's partner engagement — how Aviso drives immediate actions without disrupting existing processes. Quantify adoption with time-to-value metrics from comparable rollouts.

  • Security, compliance & integration gauntlet

    HPE stakeholders have pre-flagged integration, data security, and implementation as priority evaluation criteria. Multiple attendees will probe this from different angles — Bobby on governance, Dave on SOX/financial controls, Gustav on scalability. Compile SOC 2, ISO 27001, and data-encryption documentation in advance. Prepare the CRM/ERP integration architecture diagram showing connections to HPE's existing systems. Address data residency, SAML/SSO, encryption at rest and in transit. The 90-minute window is tight — front-load the security narrative in the first segment to neutralize anxiety before the deep-dive demos begin.
